I'll analyze the webpage for WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.1.1 compliance by examining all images and their descriptions. Let me start by accessing the webpage to understand its structure and identify all image elements.

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re

# Fetch the webpage
url = "https://www.indiatimes.com/trending/pandora-looks-better-than-ever-but-netizens-hail-the-visuals-of-avatar-fire-and-ash-yet-slam-storytellingcheck-out-reactions/articleshow/126069886.html"
headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36'
}

response = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
```

Now let me examine the page structure and identify all image elements:

```python
# Find all image elements
img_elements = soup.find_all('img')
print(f"Total image elements found: {len(img_elements)}")

# Let's examine the structure of the page to understand the content
print("\nPage title:", soup.title.string if soup.title else "No title")

# Look for main content area
main_content = soup.find('article') or soup.find('main') or soup.find('div', class_=re.compile(r'content|article|main'))
if main_content:
    print(f"Main content area found: {main_content.name} with classes: {main_content.get('class', [])}")
```

```python
# Let's examine the first few images to understand the pattern
for i, img in enumerate(img_elements[:10]):
    print(f"\nImage {i+1}:")
    print(f"  src: {img.get('src', 'No src')}")
    print(f"  alt: {img.get('alt', 'No alt')}")
    print(f"  class: {img.get('class', 'No class')}")
    print(f"  parent: {img.parent.name if img.parent else 'No parent'}")
```

Let me also check for other non-text content like videos, iframes, and canvas elements:

```python
# Check for other non-text content

iframe_elements = soup.find_all('iframe')

embed_elements = soup.find_all('embed')
object_elements = soup.find_all('object')
```

Now let me analyze the images more thoroughly, particularly focusing on those in the main content area:

```python
# Let's look for images in what appears to be the article content
# Based on the URL structure, this is likely an article page
article_images = []

# Try to find the article container
article_container = soup.find('article') or \
                   soup.find('div', {'class': re.compile(r'article|content|story|post')}) or \
                   soup.find('div', {'id': re.compile(r'article|content|story|post')})

if article_container:
    article_images = article_container.find_all('img')
    print(f"\nImages in article content: {len(article_images)}")
else:
    print("\nCould not identify article container clearly")
    # Let's look for images with more context
    article_images = [img for img in img_elements if img.get('src') and 'article' in img.get('src', '') or 
                     any(parent.get('class') and any(cls in str(parent.get('class')).lower() 
                     for cls in ['article', 'content', 'story', 'post']) 
                     for parent in img.parents)]

# Analyze article images
print("\nAnalyzing article images:")
for i, img in enumerate(article_images[:15]):  # Limit to first 15 for analysis
    alt_text = img.get('alt', '')
    src = img.get('src', '')
    print(f"\nImage {i+1}:")
    print(f"  Alt text: '{alt_text}'")
    print(f"  Src: {src[:80]}..." if len(src) > 80 else f"  Src: {src}")
    print(f"  Has alt: {bool(alt_text)}")
    print(f"  Alt length: {len(alt_text)}")
```

I'll analyze the BBC article URL for WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.1.1 compliance. Let me start by loading the page and examining its content.

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re

# Load the BBC article
url = "https://www.bbc.com/culture/article/20251215-avatar-fire-and-ash-review"
headers = {
    'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36'
}

try:
    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
    response.raise_for_status()
    
    # Parse the HTML
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
    
    # Find all image elements
    images = soup.find_all(['img', 'picture', 'figure', 'svg'])
    
    print(f"Total image-related elements found: {len(images)}")
    
    # Let's examine the structure more carefully
    print("\n=== Analyzing page structure ===")
    
    # Look for main content area
    main_content = soup.find('main') or soup.find('article') or soup.find(class_=re.compile(r'article|content|main'))
    
    if main_content:
        print(f"Found main content area")
        # Get images within main content
        content_images = main_content.find_all(['img', 'picture', 'figure'])
        print(f"Images in main content: {len(content_images)}")
        
        # Let's examine each image in detail
        print("\n=== Detailed image analysis ===")
        for i, img in enumerate(content_images[:10], 1):  # Limit to first 10 for initial analysis
            print(f"\nImage {i}:")
            print(f"Tag: {img.name}")
            
            if img.name == 'img':
                print(f"src: {img.get('src', 'No src')}")
                print(f"alt: {img.get('alt', 'No alt attribute')}")
                print(f"class: {img.get('class', 'No class')}")
            elif img.name == 'picture':
                print("Picture element found")
                img_tags = img.find_all('img')
                for img_tag in img_tags:
                    print(f"  Nested img src: {img_tag.get('src', 'No src')}")
                    print(f"  Nested img alt: {img_tag.get('alt', 'No alt')}")
            elif img.name == 'figure':
                print("Figure element found")
                fig_img = img.find('img')
                if fig_img:
                    print(f"  Figure img src: {fig_img.get('src', 'No src')}")
                    print(f"  Figure img alt: {fig_img.get('alt', 'No alt')}")
                fig_caption = img.find('figcaption')
                if fig_caption:
                    print(f"  Caption: {fig_caption.get_text(strip=True)[:100]}...")
    
    # Also check for decorative images that might be marked with empty alt
    print("\n=== Checking for decorative images ===")
    all_imgs = soup.find_all('img')
    decorative_count = 0
    informative_count = 0
    missing_alt_count = 0
    
    for img in all_imgs:
        alt = img.get('alt', None)
        if alt == '':
            decorative_count += 1
        elif alt is None:
            missing_alt_count += 1
        elif alt and alt.strip():
            informative_count += 1
    
    print(f"Total img tags: {len(all_imgs)}")
    print(f"Informative images (with alt text): {informative_count}")
    print(f"Decorative images (alt=''): {decorative_count}")
    print(f"Images missing alt attribute: {missing_alt_count}")
    
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading page: {e}")
```
